initial_query: Hello, this is Nisha from a marketplace vendor team. We are hiring remote catalog reviewers. You will start with 3 tiny tasks and receive small wallet credits. To unlock the next batch, top up ₹1,200 in USDT and connect your account to our Telegram bot. No interview needed; only today’s batch is open.

recruiter_profile.content: Nisha Rao | WhatsApp recruiter alias | Bio: 'Remote task openings, quick payout, batch closes tonight.'

company_profile.content: No verified company is disclosed. The message uses a WhatsApp number and a Telegram bot instead of a real employer page. FTC guidance says task scams start with unexpected messages about online work, show fake earnings, and later ask victims to deposit their own money, usually in crypto; Scamwatch warns that any job requiring payment to make money could be a scam.

thread_history.content: Sender: 'Complete task 1 and 2 to see your earnings grow.' Candidate: 'Can I withdraw now?' Sender: 'Add the USDT top-up first so finance can release the larger payout.'

job_post_comments.content: A colleague replies: 'This looks like a task scam — they pay a tiny amount first and then ask for more money.'

initial_query: Telegram post in @TalentOpsVault: 'AI QA annotation contract, 20 seats only. Join the bot, complete a 2-minute voice screening, and upload your ID after the bot assigns your project code. The client name will be revealed after shortlist.'

recruiter_profile.content: Admin @project_code_bot | Telegram channel owner | Bio: 'Shortlist first, client later, daily contract work.'

company_profile.content: No verified employer is disclosed. The post only mentions a Telegram bot and says the client name will be shared later. Indeed flags postings that direct applicants to suspicious external sites or unapproved channels and that ask for sensitive personal information too early in the process.

thread_history.content: User: 'Which company is this for and is there a written contract?' Admin: 'That comes after the voice screening. Send your ID first so the bot can assign your project code.'

job_post_comments.content: Only emoji reactions and 'DM sent' replies are visible. No public verification from other users.

initial_query: Post in @GoogleTasksIndia: 'Google India AI Division is onboarding remote Data Labeling Associates for a short-term project. Earn ₹1,200–₹2,500 per completed batch. No experience required. We will pay your first batch earnings within 24 hours to prove the system works. DM @google_hr_india to get your task dashboard link. Only 30 spots remaining today.'

recruiter_profile.content: Admin @google_hr_india | Telegram handler | Bio: 'Official Google AI project coordinator. Fast onboarding, same-day payouts, limited seats.'

company_profile.content: Google India / Google Careers | Industry: Technology | Website: https://careers.google.com/ | About: Google hires through its official careers portal and verified partner agencies. Google India has explicitly stated that it does not recruit via Telegram channels, DMs, or WhatsApp, and never runs 'task batch' payment schemes. Small initial payouts used to build trust before escalating deposit demands are a hallmark of pig-butchering style job fraud.

thread_history.content: User DMs @google_hr_india: 'How do I start?' Admin: 'Complete your profile form and we send ₹300 to your UPI as a welcome bonus.' Admin (next day): 'Your first batch is ready. To unlock batches above ₹1,000 you must upgrade to a Tier-2 task slot. Pay ₹2,499 via UPI. Refundable after 5 completed batches.'

job_post_comments.content: Channel has 14,000 members. Comments show only emojis and 'payment received' screenshots. No verifiable names or profiles behind the testimonials. One pinned message says: 'Do not trust people who say this is fake. They are competitors.'

initial_query: Hi, I am Sneha Iyer calling on behalf of Wipro BPS Talent Acquisition. We shortlisted your profile from Shine.com for a Process Associate role — work from home, ₹28,000/month, no targets. Before we schedule your HR round, please share a selfie with your Aadhaar card and join our Wipro Orientation Telegram group. Reply INTERESTED to proceed.

recruiter_profile.content: Sneha Iyer | Talent Acquisition | Wipro BPS | WhatsApp: +91 97XXXXXXX83 | Bio: Profile picture shows a Wipro-branded background. No LinkedIn profile URL provided. Number is unregistered on Truecaller as a corporate line.

company_profile.content: Wipro BPS (Business Process Services) | Industry: IT and BPO services | Website: https://www.wipro.com/careers/ | About: Wipro recruits through its official careers portal, Naukri, and LinkedIn. The company does not initiate hiring via personal WhatsApp numbers or request Aadhaar selfies before a formal HR round. Redirecting candidates to Telegram groups before any verified interview is inconsistent with Wipro's documented hiring process.

thread_history.content: Sender: 'Join the Telegram group link here — [t.me/WiproOrientBPS].' Candidate: 'Can I get an email from a wipro.com address to verify?' Sender: 'Formal email comes after orientation. Telegram group is mandatory first step for BPS shortlists. Time sensitive — slot closes today evening.' Candidate: 'Is this role listed on your careers page?' Sender: 'It is a referral-quota slot, not publicly posted.'

job_post_comments.content: Forwarded in a college placement WhatsApp group. One recipient commented: 'I got the same message from a different number last week — the name was different but same Telegram group link.' Another replied: 'Wipro sent me an official email when I applied through Naukri, not WhatsApp.'

To secure your interview slot, WhatsApp your resume to +91 96XXXXXXX14 and pay a one-time refundable training material fee of ₹750 via UPI. Interview link will be shared within 2 hours of payment confirmation.

recruiter_profile.content: Divya Menon | Campus Hiring Lead | BYJU'S EdTech | WhatsApp: +91 96XXXXXXX14 | Bio: 'Fast hiring, instant slot booking, freshers can join this week itself. Pay to secure your seat.'

company_profile.content: BYJU'S (Think and Learn Pvt. Ltd.) | Industry: EdTech | Website: https://byjus.com/careers/ | About: BYJU'S posts Academic Consultant roles through its official careers site and Naukri. Despite the company undergoing financial and operational restructuring, its official recruitment process does not involve WhatsApp payments or training fees before an interview. Any post demanding pre-interview payment under a BYJU'S banner is a red flag.

thread_history.content: Candidate WhatsApps resume. Sender: 'Great profile! Pay ₹750 via UPI to ID byju.hrdesk@upi and share screenshot.' Candidate: 'Is this posted on the official site?' Sender: 'This is a direct campus quota, not on the portal. Only 6 slots left for Bengaluru. Pay now or we give your slot to the next candidate.' Candidate: 'Can I get an official email confirmation?' Sender: 'Confirmation comes after payment, that is our process.'

job_post_comments.content: 'Why is a company with BYJU'S brand asking for money? They barely pay their own employees on time.' | 'Reported this post. Looks like opportunists using the BYJU'S name during its financial troubles.' | 'I messaged the number — completely different person picked up, said nothing about BYJU'S.'
